etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + original 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") with ⺼ (肉) (ròu, "meat") = dog eating meat -> components changed to 甘 (gān, "sweet") and then 日 (rì, "sun") -> final form indicating satisfaction
etymology.interpretation: The character combines elements that originally depicted a dog consuming meat, which connotes being satisfied. Over time, the phonetic elements were altered to better convey the desired sound, with the semantic essence of satisfaction retained.
